data.title: Inca Minerals Schedules General Meeting, Emphasizes Digital Access
data.description:
Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Inca Minerals Limited ( (AU:ICG) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Inca Minerals Limited has announced a General Meeting of shareholders scheduled for July 1, 2025, at their West Perth location. The meeting will be held physically, and shareholders are encouraged to vote via proxy in advance. The company has opted not to send hard copies of the Notice of Meeting, instead making it available online. This move reflects a focus on digital accessibility and efficiency, potentially impacting shareholder engagement and participation.

More about Inca Minerals Limited

Inca Minerals Limited is a company operating in the mining industry, focusing on exploration and development of mineral resources. The company is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange under the code ASX: ICG.

Current Market Cap: A$7.88M

The latest announcement is out from Critical Resources Ltd ( (AU:CRR) ).

Critical Resources Ltd announced that all resolutions proposed at its Annual General Meeting were passed, indicating strong shareholder support for the company’s strategic direction. This development is likely to bolster the company’s operations and enhance its positioning in the critical metals industry, potentially benefiting stakeholders by ensuring continued progress in its key projects.

More about Critical Resources Ltd

Critical Resources Ltd is engaged in the exploration, development, and delivery of critical metals essential for a decarbonized future. The company is focused on its Mavis Lake Lithium Project in Ontario, Canada, and the Hall Peak Base Metals Project in New South Wales, Australia. These projects aim to expand resource potential and make new discoveries, contributing to the supply of essential minerals.

Triton Minerals Limited ( (AU:TON) ) has shared an announcement.

Triton Minerals Limited held its Annual General Meeting on May 30, 2025, where all proposed resolutions were voted on. The meeting resulted in the approval of the remuneration report and the re-election of two directors, Mr. Peng (Rod) Zhang and Mr. Xingmin (Max) Ji. However, the resolution to approve an additional 10% placement capacity was not carried. These outcomes reflect the company’s ongoing governance and strategic decisions, impacting its operational capabilities and shareholder relations.

More about Triton Minerals Limited

Triton Minerals Limited is a company operating in the mining industry, focusing on the exploration and development of graphite projects. The company aims to supply high-quality graphite to the global market, emphasizing its strategic importance in the production of batteries and other technological applications.

Tenaz Energy Corp ( (TSE:TNZ) ) has shared an announcement.

Tenaz Energy Corp. announced the results of its 2025 annual meeting of shareholders, where all proposed matters were approved. The meeting saw the election of six directors, reappointment of Deloitte LLP as auditor, and approval of all unallocated awards under the Tenaz Incentive Plan. This indicates strong shareholder support and stability in the company’s governance, potentially reinforcing its market position and investor confidence.

Valuation remains a concern due to negative earnings, but strategic acquisitions and shareholder-focused initiatives offer a hopeful outlook.

Tenaz Energy Corp. is an energy company that focuses on the acquisition and sustainable development of international oil and gas assets. data.title: KATITAS Co. Reports Strong Profit Growth Amid Modest Sales Increase
data.description:
KATITAS CO ( (KTITF) ) has released its Q4 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information KATITAS CO presented to its investors.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

KATITAS Co., Ltd. is a Japanese company listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, specializing in the refurbishing and remodeling of pre-owned single-family detached houses, primarily catering to the middle- and lower-income market. The company provides a unique ‘Fourth Option’ in housing, offering refurbished homes as an alternative to newly built, ‘as-is’ pre-owned, and rental properties.

In its latest earnings report for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025, KATITAS Co. reported a modest increase in net sales by 2.2% to ¥129,537 million, while operating profit rose significantly by 12.2% to ¥14,222 million. The company also saw a 12.4% increase in profit attributable to owners of the parent, amounting to ¥9,550 million.

Key financial metrics highlighted in the report include a 12.6% rise in ordinary profit to ¥13,876 million and an adjusted operating profit of ¥16,160 million, up 9.5% from the previous year. The company’s strategic focus on increasing the number of homes sold and expanding its inventory has contributed to these positive results. Additionally, the equity-to-asset ratio improved to 54.9%, reflecting a stronger financial position.

Looking ahead, KATITAS Co. aims to continue its growth trajectory with a forecasted 12.7% increase in net sales for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. The company plans to enhance its supply capacity and focus on product development tailored to customer needs, aiming for an operating profit of ¥20,000 million by the end of its fourth medium-term management plan.

The management remains optimistic about the future, despite economic uncertainties, and is committed to maintaining a progressive dividend policy with a payout ratio of at least 50%, reflecting its dedication to shareholder returns.

data.title: OPAP S.A. Reports Strong Q1 2025 Financial Performance
data.description:
Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics ( (GOFPY) ) has released its Q1 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics presented to its investors.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

The Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics, known as OPAP S.A., is a leading gaming operator in Greece, specializing in lottery, sports betting, and online gaming services. The company is renowned for its diversified gaming portfolio and strong presence in both retail and online channels.

OPAP S.A. has reported a robust start to 2025, with a significant increase in its financial performance for the first quarter. The company achieved a Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR) of €595 million, marking an 8.2% year-over-year growth, driven by strong online and retail performance.

Key financial highlights include a 10.7% increase in gross profit from gaming operations, reaching €250.8 million, and an 8.8% rise in EBITDA to €207.1 million. The net profit also saw an 8.9% growth to €123.4 million, with a healthy net profit margin of 20.7%. The company’s diversified earnings profile and resilient omnichannel business model have effectively mitigated risks from global economic uncertainties.

The company’s strategic focus on digital transformation and customer-centric experiences has been pivotal in its success. The online gaming sector, particularly iGaming, showed a remarkable 19.8% growth, while sports betting revenues rose by 12.8%. The company also highlighted advancements in retail digitalization through the OPAP Store App.

Looking ahead, OPAP’s management remains optimistic about achieving its 2025 growth and profitability targets. The company plans to continue leveraging innovative technology and its partnership with Allwyn to enhance customer experiences and generate shareholder value while maintaining its commitment to sustainability and social responsibility.
